messages.content:
Such phonetic conditioning is reminiscent of the phonetic regularity found 
in the southern United States.32 But on Martha's Vineyard, the distribution is more complex, and nowhere codified with the precision to be found 
in the South. Before proceeding to chart the various social factors which 
influence this feature, we should consider the influence of the linguistic 
environment, and primarily phonetic conditioning. translate into Chinese
这种音位条件反射在南部美国找到的相似的规律性。然而在玛莎葡萄园，分布更为复杂，并且没有在南方那样精确地被规范。在继续讨论可能影响这一特征的各种社会因素之前，我们应当考虑语言环境的影响，尤其是音位条件。

SEGMENTAL ENVIRONMENT. The influence of the following consonant may 
be indicated by tabulating five general articulatory dimensions:  transllate into Chinese
音段环境。通过整理五个一般的发音维度，可以表明后续辅音的影响：

If we apply these oppositions in the order given, from (a) to (e), we arrive 
at a consonant series from most favoring to least favorable to centralization, which seems to conform q'uite well to the facts:  transllate into Chinese
如果我们按照给定的顺序从（a）到（e）应用这些对立面，我们将得到一个辅音序列，从最有利于向中心化到最不利于向中心化，这似乎相当符合实际情况：

The preceding consonant follows a rather different pattern, almost the reverse, and has considerably less effect. The most favoring initial consonants 
in centralized syllables are /h, 1, r, w, m, n/, with the glottal stop allophone 
of zero heading the list. Thus the most favored words are right, wife, night, 
light, nice, life, house, out. translate into Chinese
前面的辅音遵循一个相当不同的模式，几乎是相反的，并且有显著较小的影响。在中心化音节中最有利的起始辅音是 /h, l, r, w, m, n/，其中零的声门塞音变体排在首位。因此，最受青睐的单词包括 right, wife, night, light, nice, life, house, out。

LEXICAL CONSIDERATIONS. A few special words are given greater centralization than their phonetic form or prosodic position would usually 
account for. An example is sliding, meaning coasting with a small sled. It 
may be that confusion with an alternant form sledding is responsible, or 
that words which originate in childhood, and are seldom spelled, are more 
prone to centralization.  translate into Chinese
词汇考虑。一些特殊的单词比其语音形式或韵律位置通常所表明的更倾向于向中心化。一个例子是sliding，意指用小雪橇滑行。这可能是因为它与另一个形式sledding混淆所致，或者是源于童年且很少被拼写的单词更易于中心化。

7. Distribution by age and time 
The over-all degree of centralization for each speaker is expressed by the 
mean of the numerical values of the grades of each instance listed on the 
chart. Thus on Figure 4, the centralization index for /ai/ (CI /ai/), is 0.75, 
and the index for fauf (CI /auf), is 0.39. We can then find the mean CI for 
any group of persons by averaging the CI for the members of the group. 
We may first wish to see if centralization varies with the age level of the 
speaker. Table 2 indicates that it does. translate into Chinese

7. 年龄与时间的分布
每位发言者的中心化总体程度通过对图表上列出的每个实例的等级数值求平均而得到表示。因此，在图 4 上，/ai/ 的中心化指数（CI /ai/）是 0.75，而 /auf/ 的指数（CI /auf）是 0.39。我们可以通过计算组内成员的 CI 平均值来找到任何人群组的平均 CI。我们首先可能希望看看中心化是否随着发言者的年龄层次变化。表格 2 表明确实如此。

When this opinion is viewed in the light of Bloomfield's entire discussion of 
phonetic change, it appears to be strongly motivated by arguments for the 
absolute regularity of sound change. Bloomfield wishes to show that such 
change is quite autonomous, "a gradual favoring of some non-distinctive 
variants and a disfavoring of others," and quite distinct from the normal 
fluctuation of non-distinctive forms, "at all times highly variable." Yet 
since direct observations will always pick up this normal fluctuation, 
"even the most accurate phonetic record of a language at any one time 
could not tell us which phonemes were changing."36 The changes we do 
observe are likely to be the effects of borrowing and analogic change.  translate into Chinese

当这种看法结合布卢姆菲尔德对音变全面讨论的背景来看时，它似乎强烈受到了声音变化绝对规律性的论证所激发。布卢姆菲尔德希望展示，这种变化是相当自发的，“某些非区别性变体逐渐受到青睐，而其他变体则相反”，并且与非区别性形式的正常波动完全不同，“在任何时候都高度可变”。然而，由于直接观察总是会捕捉到这种正常的波动，“即使是对某一时期语言最准确的音位记录也无法告诉我们哪些音位正在变化。”36 我们观察到的变化可能是借用和类推变化的效果。

Hockett, while recognizing the possibility of divergent views, has further 
refined the doctrine of imperceptible changes as a basic mechanism of 
linguistic change. Movements of the center of the normal distribution of 
random variations are, for all practical purposes, not subject to direct 
observation,37 while the cruder forms of change which are observed must 
be due to minor mechanisms. Weinreich has pointed out the theoretical 
limitations of this position ;3s here we may profitably examine the result of 
applying such neo-grammarian thinking to empirical observations. 
The prototoype of close studies of sound change in a single community 
is Gauchat's 1899 investigation of the patois of Charmey, in Frenchspeaking Switzerland.39 Gauchat observed and tabulated differences in six 
phonological features in the speech of three generations: speakers over 
60 years old, those between 30 and 60, and those under 30. Hermann returned to the scene in 1929, one generation later, to investigate four of these 
features: his results confirmed the interpretation of Gauchat's data as 
evidence for historical change, since three of the four had advanced considerably in the same direction. Yet Hermann also showed that real time 
depth is essential for an accurate view, since the fourth feature had not 
changed since 1903, and was apparently subject to a number of conflicting 
influences. translate into Chinese

霍基特在承认可能存在不同观点的同时，进一步细化了微不可察的变化作为语言变化基本机制的教义。实际上，随机变化的正态分布中心的运动并不受直接观察的影响，37而观察到的较粗略的变化形式必须归因于次要机制。韦因赖希指出了这种立场的理论局限性；38在这里，我们可以有益地检视将这种新语法主义思想应用于实证观察的结果。

对一个社区中声音变化的近距离研究的典型例子是高谢在1899年对法语区瑞士沙尔美乡村方言的调查。39 高谢观察并记录了三代人在六个音韵特征上的差异：超过60岁的发言者，30至60岁之间的人，以及30岁以下的人。赫尔曼在1929年，一个世代之后，回到现场调查其中的四个特征：他的结果证实了高谢数据作为历史变化证据的解释，因为四个特征中有三个在同一方向上有了相当大的进展。然而，赫尔曼也展示了实时深度对于准确视角是必不可少的，因为第四个特征自1903年以来没有变化，并显然受到了一些相互矛盾的影响。

If we now accept the evidence we have on hand as adequate in quantity, 
as reliable and valid, we must still decide if this particular case is an 
example of a change in community habits of speech. Two aspects of the 
question seem to make a good case for a positive answer. 
First, the records of the LANE show only moderate centralization of /ai/ 
for the four informants of 1933, aged 56 to 82. It is impossible to calibrate 
the Lowman transcription against our present scale, especially since his 
data put more stress on short utterances with stressed, elicited forms. But 
if we take the LANE symbol [ll] as equivalent to our present [ll] of Grade 2, 
it appears that these speakers had centralized norms for fai/ averaging 
about 0.86, as high as the highest point reached in our sample for age level 
60 to 90, but only half as high as the highest point for age level 30 to 60. If 
we weigh their performance against a matched group of present-day 
speakers, we may conclude that there has been an intervening drop of 
centralization before the present rise. translate into Chinese

如果我们现在认为手头上的证据在数量上是充足的、可靠的并且有效的，我们仍需要决定这个特定案例是否是社区语言习惯变化的一个例子。关于这个问题的两个方面似乎为一个肯定答复提供了有力的案例。

首先，新英格兰语言图册（LANE）的记录对于1933年的四位受访者（年龄在56至82岁之间）仅显示出/ai/的适度中心化。由于Lowman的转录无法与我们现在的标度校准，特别是因为他的数据更侧重于带有强调的、被引出的短语。但是如果我们将LANE的符号[ll]视为与我们目前的Grade 2的[ll]等同，看来这些发言者的/ai/中心化标准平均约为0.86，和我们样本中60至90岁年龄层达到的最高点一样高，但只有30至60岁年龄层最高点的一半。如果我们把他们的表现与当今的相匹配的群体进行对比，我们可以得出结论，在当前上升之前，中心化有过一次中间下降。

Secondly, the question of fau/ is conclusive. The LANE informants had 
an average rating of 0.06 for CI /au/: that is, for all practical purposes, 
zero. The record shows a steady rise in centralization of /auf-which we 
have seen to be a completely new phenomenon in Martha's Vineyard 
English-reaching indexes of well over 1.00 for most old family, up-island 
speakers, and going as high as 2.11 in one case. No postulated change in 
speaking habits with age could account for this rise. 
The fact that the amount of centralization for the very old, and the very 
young speakers, is at a minimum, shows that the effect of age cannot be 
discounted entirely, and it may indeed be a secondary factor in this distribution over age levels.  translate into Chinese

其次，/auf/的问题是决定性的。新英格兰语言图册（LANE）的受访者对CI /au/的平均评分为0.06，也就是说，实际上为零。记录显示/auf/的中心化呈稳步上升——我们已经看到这在玛莎葡萄园英语中是一个完全新现象——对于大多数老家族、上岛的发言人，其指数远超1.00，并在某一案例中高达2.11。没有一个假定的随着年龄增长的语言习惯变化能解释这种上升。

事实上，非常老和非常年轻的发言者的中心化程度处于最低点，表明年龄效应不能完全被忽视，实际上可能是这种跨年龄层的分布中的一个次要因素。

8. Possible explanations for a rise -in centralization 
So far, our discussion of centralization, the dependent variable under 
study, has been merely descriptive. As we turn to the problem of explanation, we are faced with the question of what independent variables to examine. Certainly the structural parallelism of /ai/ and fauf is significant 
here.43 Let us assume for the moment that centralization declined to a low 
point in the late 1930's, and then, after the war, began to rise. At this point 
we find that a rising first element of fai/ carries the first element of /au/ with 
it. Such a change in direction would seem to give us a plausible explanation 
for the parallelism being called into play at this time, rather than the 
assumption that it suddenly began to operate after a three hundred year 
hiatus.  translate into Chinese

8. 中心化上升的可能解释
迄今为止，我们对中心化——本研究下的因变量——的讨论仅仅是描述性的。当我们转向解释问题时，我们面临的问题是要检查哪些独立变量。当然，/ai/和/auf/的结构平行性在这里是重要的。43让我们暂时假设中心化在1930年代末降至一个低点，然后在战后开始上升。在这一点上，我们发现/ai/的上升首元素带动了/auf/的首元素一起上升。这种方向的变化似乎为这种平行性在此时被调动起来提供了一个合理的解释，而不是假设它在三百年的间歇后突然开始起作用。

There remains the prior question, that of explaining (or giving a larger 
context for) the general rise of centralization on the island. Why should 
Martha's Vineyard turn its back on the history of the English language? 
I believe that we can find a specific explanation if we study the detailed 
configuration of this sound change against the social forces which affect 
the life of the island most deeply. 
If we choose a purely psychological explanation, or one based only on 
phonological paradigms, we have as much as said that social variables such 
as occupation, income, education, social aspirations, attitudes, are beside 
the point. We could only prove such a claim by cross-tabulating the independent social variables, one at a time, with the degree of centralization, 
and showing that any greater-than-chance correlations are spurious.  translate into Chinese

依然有一个先决问题，那就是解释（或为）岛上中心化普遍上升提供一个更大的背景。为什么玛莎葡萄园会背离英语语言的历史走向？我相信，如果我们研究这种声音变化的详细格局与最深刻影响岛屿生活的社会力量的关系，我们可以找到一个具体的解释。

如果我们选择一个纯粹心理学的解释，或仅基于音系模式的解释，我们就等于说职业、收入、教育、社会抱负、态度等社会变量是无关紧要的。我们只能通过一次又一次地将独立的社会变量与中心化程度相交叉，并展示任何大于偶然相关性的联系都是虚假的，来证明这样的主张。

The reason for this economic pressure, and the resulting dependency on 
the tourist trade, is not hard to find. There is no industry on Martha's 
Vineyard. The island reached its peak in the great days of the whaling 
industry; for a time, commercial fishing in the local waters buoyed up the 
economy, but the run of fish is no longer what it used to be. Large scale 
fishing is now out of New Bedford on the Grand Banks. Farming and 
dairying have declined sharply because of the ferry rate, which raises the 
cost of fertilizer but lowers the profit on milk.  translate into Chinese
造成这种经济压力和对旅游业依赖的原因并不难找到。玛莎葡萄园岛上没有工业。该岛在捕鲸业的黄金时代达到了巅峰；一度，当地水域的商业捕鱼撑起了经济，但如今渔获量已不再如昔。大规模的捕鱼现在是在新贝德福德沿大西洋大堤进行。农业和乳业因为渡轮费用大幅下降，化肥成本增加但牛奶利润降低。

The 1960 Census shows us that the island's labor force of two thousand 
souls is heavily occupied with service trades. Only 4% are in manufacturing, one seventh of the state average. Five percent are in agriculture, 2.5% in fishing, and 17% in construction; these percentages are five, ten 
and three times as high as those for the state as a whole. 46 
These economic pressures must be clearly delineated in order to assess 
the heavy psychological pressures operating on the Vineyarders of old 
family stock. Increasing dependence on the summer trade acts as a threat 
to their personal independence. The more far-seeing Vineyarders can envisage the day when they and their kind will be expropriated as surely as 
the Indians before them. They understand that the vacation business cannot help but unbalance the economy, which produces far too little for the 
summer trade, but far too much for the winter. Yet it is very hard for the 
Vineyarder not to reach for the dollar that is lying on the table, as much as 
he may disapprove of it. We have already noted that many Vineyarders 
move out of their own homes to make room for summer people. translate into Chinese

1960年人口普查告诉我们，岛上两千名劳动力中有很大一部分人从事服务行业。只有4%的人在制造业工作，这是该州平均水平的七分之一。农业占5%，渔业占2.5%，建筑业占17%；这些百分比是该州整体水平的五倍、十倍和三倍。46

为了评估对老家族库存的玛莎葡萄园居民造成的沉重心理压力，这些经济压力必须被清晰地描绘出来。对夏季生意的日益依赖被视为对他们个人独立性的威胁。有远见的玛莎葡萄园居民可以预见到有一天他们和他们的同类人将像之前的印第安人一样被剥夺。他们明白，度假业势必打破经济的平衡，因为它为夏季生意生产的远远不够，但对冬季而言又远远过剩。然而，对玛莎葡萄园居民来说，不去拿桌子上摆着的美元是非常困难的，尽管他们可能不赞同这样做。我们已经注意到，很多玛莎葡萄园居民让出自己的房子给夏日游客住。

You people who come down here to Martha's Vineyard don't understand the 
background of the old families of the island ... strictly a maritime background and 
tradition ... and what we're interested in, the rest of America, this part over here 
across the water that belongs to you and we don't have anything to do with, has 
forgotten all about .... 
I think perhaps we use entirely different ... type of English language ... think 
differently here on the island ... it's almost a separate language within the English 
language. translate into Chinese

你们这些来到玛莎葡萄园的人不了解岛上老家族的背景……完全是一个海事背景和传统……以及我们感兴趣的东西，美国其他地方，就是这边隔着水的部分属于你们的，而我们跟它没有什么联系，已经全部忘记了……

我想也许我们使用的是完全不同的……英语类型……在岛上的思考方式也不同……它几乎是英语语言中的一种独立的语言。
